source.title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults with varicose veins, lower-limb symptoms, skin changes, venous ulceration, or venous insufficiency context.
source.setting: Primary care and vascular-service recognition and referral assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, eczema, and chronic-venous-insufficiency context. It does not diagnose stasis dermatitis or venous eczema and does not generate referral or treatment. This batch additionally reviews adult symptom, complication, referral, duplex and pregnancy recommendations while preserving the previously registered chronic-venous-skin and ulcer-history sections. No diagnosis, imaging or treatment is generated. exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1.2.2 — symptomatic veins and lower-limb skin changes

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, and eczema considered by the clinician in chronic venous insufficiency context.

items.rationale: The exact venous-symptom and skin-change section supports documenting a stasis-dermatitis review, interval change, lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itch, clinician skin inspection, distribution, pigmentation or eczema and impact without asserting chronic venous insufficiency.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting a current or healed venous leg ulcer and an already made vascular referral without generating diagnosis, referral, urgency, or treatment.
